critic_raw:
Issue WEAR-1 — Setting shift from ICU telemetry to consumer wearable; no wearable validation.
Issue WEAR-2 — Stroke prevention claim requires multi-step pathway evidence not present.
Issue WEAR-3 — Prevalence and workup shift; false positive burden in screening context unanalyzed.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 (Critic output visible):
WEAR-1: Valid — wearable validation required. Empirically open.
WEAR-2: Valid — stroke prevention pathway evidence absent. Empirically open.
WEAR-3: Valid — prevalence and false positive analysis absent. Concede.
Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

debate_rounds.critic_move: Accept empirical test for both. Proposed test: wearable device validation study (wrist ECG or PPG, ambulatory, motion conditions); separately, near-term pathway study measuring alert-to-confirmation rate.
debate_rounds.defender_move: Accept. Two-stage testing plan is the right approach. Stage 1 is feasible within 6-12 months; Stage 2 is feasible within 12-24 months. The stroke outcome study is a longer-term commitment.
debate_rounds.resolution: RESOLVED: all three issues — empirical test agreed

adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_wearable_screen_307 — Multi-Round Run 2

Consistent with Run 1.

Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED

empirical_test.measure: Stage 1: wearable validation on ambulatory participants; Stage 2: alert-to-care-seeking pathway study
empirical_test.success_criterion: acceptable sensitivity/specificity on wearable signal; majority of alert recipients seek confirmatory evaluation
empirical_test.failure_criterion: significant performance degradation; most alert recipients do not seek care
